headline: HLP embedded at protocol level; no traditional smart contracts discovered for core vault

findings.text: Hyperliquid L1 operates HyperEVMScan block explorer; no HLP-specific name tags found (protocol-level implementation)
Official docs at https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs confirm HLP existence but do not list contract addresses; HLP embedded in HyperCore
No audit links recorded in pinned context; docs inaccessible (permission denied)
GitHub org https://github.com/hyperliquid-dex exists; contracts repo fetch blocked (permission denied); SDKs and node software published
Single chain (Hyperliquid L1) in pinned context; cross-chain N/A
No factory enumeration needed; HLP is protocol-native vault, not a factory
Assistance fund system address 0xfefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefe identified with role 'assistance fund' and actor_class 'governance'
Single address trace to fetched evidence; protocol-level vault does not map to traditional smart contract addresses

rationale.verdict: HLP is implemented at the Hyperliquid L1 consensus layer (HyperCore), not as a smart contract on HyperEVM. Core vault operations are protocol-native and do not expose discoverable smart contract addresses. Only the assistance fund system address (0xfefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefe) was identified from on-chain sources. Without traditional contract deployments, systematic discovery of control addresses is not feasible via block explorers or GitHub build artifacts. GitHub source access was blocked; audit links absent from pinned context.

evidence.shows: HLP is a protocol vault providing liquidity through market making; community-owned; fully embedded in HyperCore
Official Hyperliquid documentation; HyperCore is the trading engine with on-chain order books; HyperEVM is separate EVM-compatible layer
Official Hyperliquid GitHub organization; hosts node software, SDKs, and contracts repository
Block explorer for Hyperliquid HyperEVM; tracks smart contracts on EVM layer; no protocol-level HLP vault contract visible
Confirms assistance fund address 0xfefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefe operates on-chain as part of L1 execution with validator quorum requirement

unknowns: D1-offchain: HyperEVMScan and HurrScan block explorer search for 'Hyperliquid HLP' protocol tag did not yield additional contract addresses; core HLP vault is protocol-level, not smart contract
D2-offchain: Deployed contracts documentation page at docs site inaccessible (permission denied); GitBook fetch failed
D3-offchain: Audit links absent from pinned context; audit documentation page inaccessible
D4-offchain: GitHub contracts repository access blocked; cannot enumerate deployment artifacts or script outputs
D6: HLP factory enumeration N/A; HLP is monolithic protocol vault, not a factory with children

Control-surface mapping: HLP core vault operations reside at consensus layer; traditional smart contract control addresses (owner, admin, proxy_admin) do not apply to protocol-native implementation; only assistance fund address (0xfefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefe) identified as governance-controlled fund on-chain

protocol_metadata.about: Hyperliquidity Provider (HLP) is Hyperliquid's protocol-native vault that provides liquidity to the exchange through market-making strategies, performs liquidations, and supplies USDC to Earn mechanisms. Unlike traditional DeFi vaults, HLP is embedded directly into Hyperliquid's L1 consensus layer (HyperCore) and operates deterministically across all validator nodes. Community members deposit USDC and share in trading fees and strategy profits; deposits are subject to a 4-day lock-up period.
